title: citrus × aurantium subf. cyathifera
intro: Citrus × aurantium subf. cyathifera, also known as bitter orange, is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family and is a hybrid of the sour orange (Citrus × aurantium) and the Seville orange (Citrus × aurantium var. aurantium). The medicinal properties of bitter orange are attributed to its essential oils, which contain limonene, beta-pinene, and beta-myrcene, and have been used to treat various health issues due to their antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of bitter orange include improving digestion, reducing anxiety and stress, and aiding in weight loss, as well as possessing antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. Bitter orange is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or essential oil for medicinal purposes, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation.
